c-suite_officer: John Kordash: SVP, Cloud and CIO
exact_hq_location: 200 Ballardvale Street, Building 1, Suite 100, Wilmington, MA 01887, United States
industry_and_segment: Supply Chain Management, specifically for the Life Sciences and Healthcare industries, focusing on pharmaceutical traceability, serialization, and orchestration.
no_of_users: 291,000+ authenticated and onboarded network members; 1,800+ life sciences and healthcare customers
key_positioning: World's largest Agentic Business Network, enabling agentic supply chain orchestration and end-to-end supply chain execution with a single integration, promoting an 'Integrate Once, Interoperate with Everyone™' model.
revenue_model: SaaS network creation platform, offering cloud-based solutions likely on a subscription or service model, including network onboarding and continuous regulatory compliance.

current_problems_struggling_with.description: Drug shortages are a top challenge, with a significant percentage of respondents experiencing them during the pandemic.
current_problems_struggling_with.user_type: Supply Chain Leaders (Pharmaceutical companies, wholesale distributors, hospitals, pharmacies)
effect: Leads to limited access to critical medicines.
Impacts both COVID-19 related and unrelated treatments.

current_problems_struggling_with.description: Concerns about drug counterfeiting, diversion, and theft have greatly increased due to global disruptions.
current_problems_struggling_with.user_type: Supply Chain Leaders (Pharmaceutical companies, wholesale distributors, hospitals, pharmacies)
effect: Jeopardizes patient safety and integrity of pharmaceuticals.
Contributes to an estimated $75 billion in lost industry revenue each year.

current_problems_struggling_with.description: Poor end-to-end visibility and a lack of agility are inhibiting pharmaceutical supply chains from being resilient to disruptions.
effect: Makes it difficult to accurately plan supply (65% of respondents).
Hampers efforts to create patient-centric supply chains.
Leads to painfully slow issue resolution with upstream and downstream partners (over 40 days for 45% of respondents).

current_problems_struggling_with.description: Lengthy incident resolution processes and managing supply chain partners with recurring problems are major challenges for manufacturers.
current_problems_struggling_with.user_type: Manufacturing Organizations (Managers, Directors, VPs, C-level executives)
effect: Slows down the entire supply chain process.
Puts critical information at risk of going unrecorded.
Escalates shipment delays, material shortages, and quality defects into disruptions.

current_problems_struggling_with.description: Reliance on ad hoc processes and unstructured collaboration tools (emails, spreadsheets) leads to ineffective supply chain issue management.
effect: Creates a lack of institutional memory to support future improvement.
Results in poor coordination and communication with supply chain partners.
Jeopardizes supply chain operations by slowing issue resolution.

differentiators.feature: World's Largest Agentic Business Network for Life Sciences and Healthcare
effect: Enables end-to-end orchestration and real-time information sharing across 310,000+ partners.
Provides a trusted, regulated transaction backbone for the supply chain.

differentiators.feature: Integrate Once, Interoperate with Everyone™ Model
effect: Eliminates the need for costly and cumbersome point-to-point integrations.
Facilitates seamless data exchange and collaboration.
Reduces risks and costs while improving supply chain visibility.
Executes and coordinates mission-critical supply chain operations alongside human teams.
Operates as permissioned, governed users with full auditability and enterprise-grade controls.
Accelerates digitalization of supply networks by providing instant, context-aware insights.
Empowers professionals to optimize operations and solve complex challenges confidently.

differentiators.feature: Specialized Focus on Pharmaceutical Supply Chain
effect: Allows for a deeper understanding of industry complexities and faster adaptation to evolving regulations.
Strengthens its competitive position through intellectual property in serialization data management.

user_complaints.issue: Poor management, toxic politics, and lack of support in professional services team.
effect: Creates a negative work environment.
Leads to micro-management and stress.

user_complaints.issue: Lack of flexibility with work-from-home policy and too much work, negatively impacting work-life balance.
user_complaints.user_type: Internal Staff (Technical Support Engineer, Design Operations Lead)
effect: Leads to dissatisfaction among employees.
Causes stress and potential burnout.

user_complaints.issue: Unclear product strategy and difficulty in prioritizing decisions.
effect: Can lead to inefficiencies and lack of direction.
Potentially impacts project execution and innovation.

user_complaints.issue: Lack of job security, particularly in sales roles, described as a 'filler job'.
effect: High employee turnover.
Difficulty in retaining talent.

user_complaints.issue: Product is not stable, and management is inexperienced (older review, but noted as a historical concern).
effect: Challenges with product performance and customer satisfaction.
Perception of the company as a 'new startup' despite its founding year.

strategic_moves.move: Deepened strategic partnership with Kinaxis to synchronize supply chain planning with real-time execution.
effect: Links planning decisions with live partner data and digitalized multienterprise processes.
Enables faster, more coordinated supply chain operations and continuous, network-wide planning.

strategic_moves.move: Launched OPUS Agents and created the 'Agentic Business Network' as the next era of supply chain execution.
effect: Introduces no-code enterprise AI agents that execute work across the end-to-end supply network.
Establishes a digital network-powered model where AI agents operate in concert with human teams.

strategic_moves.move: Focused on scaling agentic orchestration across the global life sciences and healthcare supply chain.
effect: Aims to capitalize on the generational opportunity presented by digitalization and AI deployment.
Builds upon a foundation of trusted network data, regulated infrastructure, and end-to-end orchestration.

strategic_moves.move: Transformation into a platform company with the OPUS platform's no-code digitalization tools and an expanding ecosystem of partners.
effect: Enables the company to serve 10-100 times more customers and trading partners.
Delivers richer capabilities that empower the orchestration of all business processes for end-to-end supply chain digitalization.

strategic_moves.move: Launched Amadeus, the first Generative AI Assistant for accelerating the digitalization of supply networks on the OPUS platform.
effect: Provides instant, context-aware, and accurate insights from a unified content repository.
Optimizes operations and solves complex challenges with greater confidence.

strategic_moves.move: Successful execution of the U.S. Drug Supply Chain Security Act (DSCSA) dispenser requirement.
effect: Established a trusted, regulated transaction backbone across the life sciences ecosystem.
Connected manufacturers, wholesalers, dispensers, and 3PLs across interoperable enterprise systems.

milestones: Founded in 2009 in North Reading, United States.
Raised a total of $230M in funding over 8 rounds.
Grew to over 291,000 authenticated and onboarded network members.

Successfully executed the U.S. Drug Supply Chain Security Act (DSCSA) dispenser requirement in 2025, activating ~300 new DSCSA live links per week and processing billions of serialized EPCIS transactions.
Launched OPUS Agents, no-code enterprise AI agents, and the Agentic Business Network in April 2026.
Named a leader in IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Multienterprise Supply Chain Commerce Network 2025 Vendor Assessment.
Recognized in RXinsider's 2026 Pharmacy500 for powering digital network orchestration.
CEO Shabbir Dahod received Lifetime Achievement in Supply & Demand Chain Executive’s 'Pros to Know' Awards in 2024.
Received a Merit Award in 2023 for a pioneering AI-driven solution to predict drug shortages.
Awarded a Pharma Manufacturing award in 2023 for its Multienterprise Information Network Tower (MINT) solution.
new_features_launched: OPUS Agents (no-code enterprise AI agents) for end-to-end supply network execution (April 2026).
Kinaxis Network Collaboration by TraceLink, integrating Kinaxis Maestro™ with real-time trading partner data (April 2026).
Amadeus, the first Generative AI Assistant for accelerating the digitalization of supply networks (January 2025).
Multienterprise Information Network Tower (MINT) for network exchange of purchase orders, invoices, inventory status, forecasts, and chargebacks (2025).
No-code, drag-and-drop capabilities on the OPUS platform for configuring multienterprise solutions and dashboards (January 2025).
Smart Inventory Tracker (SIT) mobile solution for handling serialized products in warehouse operations (Latest updates Oct 2025, March 2026).
Process Orchestration for Empowered Teams (POET) as a multienterprise solution for structured collaboration and issue resolution.
other_crucial_details: Operates the world's largest end-to-end digital network platform for intelligent orchestration of the supply chain.
Integrates companies and orchestrates critical, multienterprise supply chain processes to bridge enterprise silos, improving resiliency, cost, service, and predictability.

Connects manufacturers, wholesalers, dispensers, and 3PLs across interoperable enterprise systems, integrating with leading ERP, WMS, serialization, and pharmacy systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Manhattan).
Provides solutions to address critical industry challenges such as drug shortages, product recalls, real-time production and inventory visibility, and real-time demand intelligence.
